rag_rules: Fix type mismatches in AGREE expressions by ensuring left and right sides of '=' have identical types.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts to avoid 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always match the declared type of a helper with the type of its assigned expression.
When generating AGREE code, ensure all identifiers are visible in the current AADL scope and properly declared.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE constants and equations must declare types explicitly using int, real, or bool.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
Do not write guarantee statements in implementation scope unless they reference visible outputs and inputs.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes vs implementation annexes
